title: Ghazipur teen death: Prohibitory order issued, protests banned; visit to victim's village barred
content: The government issued a statement, saying "unverified rumours" are being spread by certain "mischievous elements" and Opposition parties are "attempting to sensationalise the matter"

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Leaders make last ditch efforts to woo voters in West Bengal
content: Former Delhi Chief Minister and Aam Aadmi Party convener Arvind Kejriwal joined the Trinamool Congress campaign in West Bengal

title: Andhra University centenary: Vice-President, Sachin Tendulkar at April 27 event
content: Vice-President and Sachin Tendulkar to attend valedictory at engineering college grounds, nearly 25,000 expected; campus illuminated, MoUs and commemorative releases planned

title: Overfed and undernourished in Delhi
content: India’s capital faces what public health professionals call the Double Burden of Malnutrition, a condition in which children over-consume calories that lack essential nutrients. Combined with the lack of physical activity, the addictive nature of junk food, and parents unaware of nutritional realities, this early undernutrition is a risk factor for obesity later in life. A report on Delhi’s obesity-without-nutrition problem that needs immediate attention

title: Despite govt’s claims, Delhi’s eateries continue to struggle to get LPG cylinders
content: Chief Minister Rekha Gupta said that the cooking distribution system across the city is functioning “smoothly” and “adequate stock is available at all times”

title: Election Commission announces tight security in 142 seats ahead of second phase of Bengal polls
content: During a high-level coordination meeting, attended by poll officials, senior police officers and commanders of central forces from three election districts under Kolkata, the ECI reviewed the preparedness in 142 constituencies that would go to polls on April 29

title: Census 2027: self-enumeration window open till April 30, public urged to use online portal
content: NTR District Joint Collector S. Ilakkiya flagged off a 5-km run in Vijayawada to spread awareness about the Census 2027 self-enumeration form, which can be filled online until April 30

title: Rahul Gandhi plans Great Nicobar visit, clearance awaited
content: The Union government is planning a ₹92,000-crore mega infrastructure project in Great Nicobar Island amid tribal communities’ concerns that they will be displaced and lose their rights over the forests there

title: Rajasthan may serve as “living lab” for farm tech companies: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma
content: He said technology had an immense potential to transform agriculture sector in the State on a large scale

title: CJI bats for structured national registry of former judges willing to serve in ADR
content: Mediation, Lok Adalats, Arbitration, Conciliation: these are not the back door to justice. For millions of Indians, they are the only door, says Chief Justice of India Surya Kant

title: Unite and resist ring road project in Amaravati, AIKS leader Ashok  Dhawale exhorts farmers
content: Questioning why provisions of the Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition Act-2013 are not being followed, particularly the requirement of Gram Sabha consultations and prior consent of landowners, he demands that farmers be given compensation at four times the market rate

title: NCSC seeks report on Mamata Banerjee's alleged 'derogatory' remark against SC community
content: The NCSC has written to the Chief Secretary of West Bengal and the Director General of Police, directing them to submit a detailed report within three days of the receipt of the notice

title: BJP attacking Bengali culture, pride: Kejriwal; Modi won’t have his way in Bengal: Tejashwi
content: AAP convenor Arvind Kejriwal and RJD leader Tejaswi Yadav are campaigning in the State in support of Trinamool Congress even as Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has launched scathing attacks on Trinamool Congress government and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Drug menace emerges as key plank in Amit Shah’s campaign for Punjab polls
content: Top sources in the BJP have told The Hindu that Mr. Shah will be visiting the State as often as once a month, through the year and to each district

title: For two women, a fight for justice in Bengal poll contest
content: Contesting from rival parties, BJP’s Ratna Debnath and CMI(M)’s Sabina Yasmin have turned personal loss into broader questions of safety, governance, and State accountability.

title: Experts seek data-led approach to tackle public health risks
content: The webinar discussed challenges from infectious diseases to NCDs, climate-linked risks, and the growing role of AI and policy in building resilient healthcare systems

title: Disqualify all seven defecting MPs, AAP says in petition to Rajya Sabha Chairman
content: Group led by Raghav Chadha claims that it fulfills required two-third strength making the merger with the BJP legal; however, the AAP has said that such a decision has to be taken by the party

title: Union government to resume talks on Ladakh on May 22
content: Amit Shah to visit Ladakh soon; the Ladakh-Centre talks hit a stalemate after violence erupted in Leh in September last year, which left four locals dead and over 80, including security personnel, injured

title: KWML awaits three ferries to extend Water Metro services to Kadamakudy
content: At present, the 20 ferries in operation across more than half a dozen routes are recording an average daily patronage of 7,500 commuters, rising to over 10,000 on weekends and public holidays

title: Provide fixed fund of at least ₹1 crore for academic chairs in universities: Prof. Baraguru Committee to government
content: While some academic chairs have a capital of ₹5 lakh, others have been given up to ₹5 crore. There is no uniformity in the amount of capital given to chairs, and this disparity should be eliminated, the committee proposed

title: Despite high prices, crowds flock to Kasimedu to buy fish
content: Vanjaram, that was selling at ₹800 a kg before the ban, was sold at ₹1,200 a kg. Paarai that was available at ₹530/kg was sold at ₹950/kg, and nethili which was sold at ₹370/kg was sold at ₹600/kg, said a trader

title: Credai Chennai gets new team of leaders for 2026-28
content: Mehul H Doshi assumed office as the 10th President, along with the newly elected office bearers and executive committee members

title: Blaze at scrapyard exposes Kochi’s fire risks again
content: The District Disaster Management Authority has initiated steps to chalk out an action plan to prevent the recurrence of such incidents

title: Palacode, Veerapandi and Kulithalai frontrunners in voter turnout since 2011
content: They saw high turnout in every Assembly poll held in the State since the 2008 delimitation. This is due to several factors, including political mobilisation on communal lines and local-level loyalties

title: WRD restores key irrigation tank that remains major water source for Vellore Corporation
content: WRD officials said that the rejuvenation of the tank was done under the Rehabilitation and Restoration of Twin Lakes scheme at a cost of ₹19 crore

title: Residents complain of suffocation after open landfill site catches fire near Gudiyatham
content: At present, 25-30 metric tonnes of garbage, including domestic waste, are dumped at the landfill site every day

title: Advertorial tracing Ramesh Chennithala’s political odyssey raises eyebrows in UDF circles
content: Advertorial, which risked being perceived as a political hagiography, appeared close on the heels of the Kerala Pradesh Congress Committee president warning the party’s rank and file against jumping the gun and promoting their choices for Congress Legislative Party leadership post on television, social and print media

title: Suspected West Nile fever cases trigger health warning
content: The cases were reported from Elanji, Eroor in Thripunithura, Pallikkara in Kizhakkambalam, Ponekkara in Edappally, and Palluruthy

title: Haryana Congress manifesto for civic polls promises clean, well-equipped and corruption-free cities
content: Laying out a 15-point plan focused on sanitation, infrastructure, safety and governance, the party promised to transform cities into “Green Cities” with extensive green belts, beautified parks and public squares

title: 'Hope of getting justice shattered': Kejriwal refuses to appear before court, writes to HC judge
content: Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal has refused to appear before the Delhi High Court in the excise policy case, citing a loss of hope for justice. He stated his decision to follow Mahatma Gandhi's path of Satyagraha and will appeal the order in the Supreme Court.

title: ‘Our math is different’: Iran warns Gulf countries of ‘fourfold’ retaliation after Trump threat
content: Iran's Vice President warned of a fourfold retaliation against nations supporting damage to its infrastructure, particularly its oil sector, following US President Trump's remarks. This threat underscores heightened tensions around Iran's energy assets amidst ongoing conflict and diplomatic efforts, including a proposed two-stage plan to the US.

title: 'Criminal decision': Rishabh Pant faces heat for Nicholas Pooran call
content: Lucknow Super Giants suffered a heart-wrenching Super Over loss to Kolkata Knight Riders, with Nicholas Pooran's first-ball dismissal drawing sharp criticism from Dale Steyn. Steyn labelled the decision to send Pooran in as 'criminal' given his poor form, highlighting the missed opportunity for LSG to secure a crucial win and their subsequent slide to the bottom of the table.

title: Before Kargil: How India handed Musharraf his first defeat in Siachen— a first-hand account
content: India secured the strategic Saltoro Ridge in 1984, leading to a fierce battle for Quaid Post. Operation Rajiv saw Indian soldiers, led by Naib Subedar Bana Singh, bravely capture the post in 1987 after intense fighting. Renamed Bana Post, its capture marked a significant Indian victory, thwarting Pakistani attempts to regain dominance.

title: After OpenAI and Anthropic, Jeff Bezos’s AI lab 'goes to' London
content: Jeff Bezos' AI lab, Project Prometheus, is reportedly in talks to lease a significant office space in London's King's Cross. This move, following substantial funding, highlights the UK's growing appeal as a hub for AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, which are also expanding their London footprints.

title: What is Iran’s latest proposal to US to end war? Ceasefire first, nuclear talks later
content: Iran has reportedly offered the US a new proposal focusing on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, postponing nuclear negotiations. This move aims to bypass internal divisions within Iran regarding nuclear concessions, though it could diminish US leverage in future talks.

title: 'Purchased' for Rs 1 lakh, toddler left on highway by bizman, wife for 'bringing bad luck'
content: A Guna couple abandoned their toddler on a highway, believing she brought bad luck. Investigations revealed the child was allegedly purchased for Rs 1 lakh through an illegal network, expanding the case into a suspected child trafficking racket. The girl was found alone and is now in the care of the Child Welfare Committee.

title: After backing Trump's child savings plan, Michael Dell's next $750 m going to hospital
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ crosses Rs 1130 cr mark amid ‘Michael,’ ‘Bhooth Bangla’ buzz
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1130 crore in India and nearing Rs 1780 crore globally after 39 days. The film demonstrated remarkable growth over its sixth weekend, outperforming competition. Despite new releases, its enduring popularity is evident in its sustained box office success.

title: Tollywood actor booked for duping NRI techie of Rs 9.35cr with marriage promise
content: A Tollywood actress and Bigg Boss contestant has been booked for allegedly defrauding a London-based software professional of Rs 9.35 crore. The victim claims the actress promised marriage and sought financial assistance for her career and personal expenses. Despite multiple attempts to finalize marriage and recover funds, the actress allegedly evaded the complainant and failed to honor repayment commitments.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: Oil prices today: Crude jumps nearly 2% as US-Iran talks stall
content: Global oil prices surged Monday, with Brent crude hitting $107.49 and WTI reaching $96.17, driven by stalled US-Iran peace talks and ongoing Strait of Hormuz disruptions.  This supply squeeze, exacerbated by restricted shipping, has led Goldman Sachs to revise its Q4 oil price forecasts upwards, signaling continued market volatility.

title: Raghuvanshi out explained: Should Pant have withdrawn the appeal?
content: With Raghuvanshi given out after a mid-dive deflection, the decision raised questions on law versus spirit and whether Rishabh Pant should have withdrawn the appeal. Rohan Gavaskar disagreed with the call, while Virender Sehwag termed it harsh. KKR later won in a Super Over.

title: Ronaldo’s 3 final career goals before retirement – Messi already ahead
content: Cristiano Ronaldo is entering the final phase of his career with three clear goals still driving him forward: a first World Cup title in 2026, the pursuit of 1,000 career goals, and the rare chance of playing alongside his son at Al Nassr. Even at 41, he remains central for club and country, shaping both legacy and milestones.

title: Dianna Russini remark on husband resurfaces amid Mike Vrabel buzz
content: Dianna Russini is facing renewed scrutiny as an old joke about marrying Kevin Goldschmidt for his “big wallet” resurfaces amid controversy involving Mike Vrabel. The remark, later clarified as humor, has been widely misinterpreted online. At the same time, rumors around leaked photos and her alleged link to Vrabel continue to circulate, though unverified, keeping the debate alive across social media and sports circles.

title: LeBron, Bronny make NBA history with first father-son playoff alley-oop
content: LeBron James and Bronny James made NBA playoff history with the league’s first father-son scoring connection, as LeBron set up an alley-oop finished by Bronny in Game 3 against the Houston Rockets. Beyond the highlight, the moment underscored LeBron’s unmatched longevity, allowing their careers to overlap in a meaningful, competitive playoff setting rather than a symbolic appearance.

title: West Bengal polls: ‘Give me votes, I’ll give you freedom,’ says PM Modi in Matua belt
content: In a powerful rally in Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi echoed Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's rallying cry, passionately urging voters in the Matua region to cast their ballots for 'freedom' from what he described as the TMC's oppressive 'jungle raj.

title: 'Nandri Chennai': Gill shares special post with yellow whistle
content: Shubman Gill celebrated Gujarat Titans’ win over Chennai Super Kings with a “Nandri Chennai” post. GT chased 159 comfortably, winning by eight wickets with 20 balls to spare. Sai Sudharsan starred with 87, supported by Gill and Jos Buttler. Earlier, Ruturaj Gaikwad made 74*, but Kagiso Rabada’s strikes restricted CSK.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for April 27 week - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: For the trading week starting April 27, 2026, Tata Steel, and Cyient DLM are the top stock picks from Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk.

title: Hyderabad comic’s show in Bengaluru disrupted over 2-year-old joke on Andhra Pradesh CM
content: Stand-up comedian Sarat Uday's show in Bengaluru was disrupted by alleged TDP protesters over past jokes about Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu. Despite an earlier apology, Uday was forced to apologize again on stage. The party president condemned the protest but cautioned against comments on sensitive issues.

title: 'Achanak se phenk diya': Rinku Singh opens up on Super Over catch
content: Rinku Singh led Kolkata Knight Riders’ victory over Lucknow Super Giants in a dramatic Super Over clash in Lucknow. After both sides tied at 155, Rinku shone with an unbeaten 83 and five catches. Sunil Narine’s decisive Super Over spell sealed it, while Rinku calmly finished the chase. His composure under pressure highlighted his growing reputation as KKR’s match-winner and a dependable finisher in high-pressure situations for the franchise consistently.

title: Delhi policeman shoots delivery worker dead, held after hours-long manhunt
content: In a tragic turn of events, a head constable from Delhi Police, Neeraj Balhara, reportedly shot and killed a young food delivery worker aged 21, while also injuring his friend in the Jafarpur Kalan area on a fateful Sunday morning. The incident unfolded as the duo was riding their bike home following a friend's birthday celebration.

title: 'It affects me a bit': Sooryavanshi opens up on criticism after duck vs SRH
content: Rajasthan Royals batter Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said he wanted to answer his critics with his bat after his poor outing against Sunrisers Hyderabad pacer Praful Hinge in their earlier IPL meeting.

title: Babar Azam 'bowled' by Shaheen's 1-year-old son; fans react - WATCH
content: Pakistan Super League star Babar Azam experienced a humorous moment off the field when Shaheen Afridi's one-year-old son, Aliyar, playfully "bowled" him. While Azam leads the PSL 2026 run charts, this adorable interaction with Afridi's child went viral, drawing widespread amusement from fans. The incident occurred after a match where Afridi's Lahore Qalandars secured a victory against Azam's Peshawar Zalmi.

title: ‘I don’t expect forgiveness’: What DC shooter’s manifesto reveals about gala attack
content: A chilling manifesto from White House Correspondents' Dinner attacker Cole Allen revealed his motives and meticulous plan.  He apologized for the chaos but deemed his actions a 'duty' against perceived 'crimes' by US leadership. Allen detailed targeting administration officials, prioritizing them by rank, while explicitly sparing FBI chief Kash Patel. His writings also criticized security lapses at the event.

title: Ice creams, chocolates may cost you more as prices of raw materials rise
content: Sweet treats like ice cream and chocolates are likely to become more expensive this summer due to supply chain disruptions caused by the West Asia war. Key ingredients like dry fruits and nuts have seen significant price hikes, impacting company margins.  Logistics and packaging costs are also rising, forcing some businesses to consider price increases to maintain quality.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 39: Ranveer Singh film chases 'Pushpa 2’s domestic record
content: On Day 39, Ranveer Singh’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is on the cusp of making history as it chases the worldwide record of SS Rajamouli’s 'Baahubali 2'. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the espionage thriller has reached a global gross of Rs. 1,772.94 crore, needing just Rs. 15.12 crore more to become the second highest-grossing Indian film ever. Domestically, the film is locked in an intense battle to dethrone 'Pushpa 2: The Rule' as the all-time India net champion.

title: 'Iran can call us': Trump rules out travel for talks as Araghchi returns to Pakistan
content: US President Donald Trump declared American officials won't travel for Iran truce talks, insisting discussions must be remote. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi returned to Islamabad, relaying Tehran's positions on nuclear issues and the Strait of Hormuz via Pakistani intermediaries. Iran maintains it won't negotiate under coercion amid ongoing US pressure.

title: 66% turnout for civic polls as people brave heat to vote in Gujarat
content: The elections are being held for 15 municipal corporations, including Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and Rajkot, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats and 260 taluka panchayats, making it one of the largest electoral exercises in the State.

title: 66% turnout for civic polls as people brave heat to vote in Gujarat
content: Polling in municipal corporations comparatively lower than in other local bodies: SEC; AAP candidate among three reported dead on polling day

title: UAE-Iran differences and India’s plans to dilute language on Israel-Palestine derail joint statement at BRICS meet
content: Deeply polarised deputy FMs meeting in Delhi spell trouble for India’s BRICS Presidency; as the language of a joint statement eluded diplomats, India issued a ‘Chair’s Statement’ that reflected the subjects discussed

title: Govt. may establish labs in varsities to train students for space sector
content: The country’s private space ecosystem has expanded rapidly, with the number of startups increasing from a single digit in 2019 to more than 400 by early 2026

title: NGT issues notice to Centre over risks from hanging glaciers in central Himalaya
content: Taking suo motu cognisance of a report on hanging glacier risks, the tribunal said it indicated violations of the Environment (Protection) Act and raised concerns over compliance with environmental norms

title: ‘Kozhikode-Kannur stretch recorded most stone attacks on trains in past three years’
content: Measures are in place to improve surveillance at vulnerable locations, says an RPF officer; the involvement of suspects linked to drug cases is also being probed

title: Trinamool running a reign of terror in West Bengal, says  Rahul Gandhi
content: Condoling the death of a Congress worker in Asansol who the party has claimed was beaten up by ‘miscreants’ linked to the ruling Trinamool, the Leader of the Opposition in Lok Sabha said his party will not bow  before ‘this politics that stains India’s non-violent tradition’

title: SIR pause on way to a billion electorate
content: The SIR has trimmed India’s electoral roll by removing absent, shifted, dead, and duplicate names, leading to record turnout percentages across States despite shrunken electorates; while the exercise strengthens the accuracy of the roll, the challenge now is to ensure it remains inclusive and free of disenfranchisement

title: Child, 7, Dies, 19 Others Hospitalised After Eating Golgappa In Jharkhand
content: A seven-year-old died and 18 others hospitalised after eating golgappa in Jharkhand's Giridih district.

title: 'I'm Not A Rapist, Paedophile": Trump On Shooter Cole Allen's 'Manifesto'
content: Trump seemed to deem the mention of 'rapist' as an insinuation about his relationship with disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein, who was not mentioned in the manifesto.

title: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Seeks Report On Petrol, Diesel Shortage
content: Amaravati, April 26 (IANS) Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu on Sunday directed District Collectors to immediately implement an action plan to address the shortage of petrol and diesel.

title: Oil Jumps, Stock Futures Slip As US-Iran Talks Stall
content: No agreement has been reached on terms to open the Strait of Hormuz which has been all but closed, sending energy prices soaring.

title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: US Must Lift Blockade Before Fresh Talks, Iran Tells Pakistan
content: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif during a phone call Saturday night that the US "should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade"

title: Trump Event Shooting LIVE Updates: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials
content: Shooting at Trump's event live updates: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after suspected gunshots were heard at the event.

title: Are Minimum Wage Laws Doing More Harm Than Good To Low-Income Workers?
content: Around 64% of Indian workers earn below the minimum wage. For almost half the workforce, hiring them would be illegal even after a 30% pay raise.

title: Trump Event Shooter Names Kash Patel As Only US Official Off Target List
content: In a manifesto sent to family members roughly ten minutes before he opened fire, Allen laid out exactly who he intended to target.

title: 10 Minutes Before Firing At Trump Event, Shooter Sent A Manifesto To Family
content: About ten minutes before Allen opened fire, he sent what has been described as an anti-Trump manifesto to members of his family.

title: India, New Zealand To Sign "Once In A Generation" Free Trade Deal Today
content: Christopher Luxon said, "New Zealand's Free Trade Agreement with India will be signed on Monday night," while sharing a video outlining the expected benefits of the deal.

title: Trump Event Shooter Mocked Hotel Security In Manifesto, Said 'Won't Use Slugs'
content: Cole Allen wrote in the manifesto that he sensed "arrogance" in the security agents as he walked into the hotel with multiple weapons without being thoroughly checked.

title: Veggies, Not Jhalmuri: Mamata Banerjee's Casual Response To PM Modi's Snack Break
content: The 71-year-old Chief Minister was seen going for the healthier options, even throwing in a bunch of fruits.

title: As Kash Patel's Past Catches Up, Report Hints At Trump Axe
content: Patel has found himself at the centre of an unwanted spotlight this week after reports emerged about two arrests during his younger years.

title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi Meets Asim Munir After Landing In Islamabad
content: There was no official word from Pakistan about the meeting which apparently focused on the ongoing peace efforts by Islamabad to arrange the second round of talks between the US and Iran.

title: West Bengal polls: PM woos Matua community, vows to fast-track citizenship
content: prime minister addresses public meeting for west Bengal assembly elections . he reiterated his resolve to fast-track Indian citizenship for its members . in a message that emphasised his association with the sect, he posted a picture of his 2019 meeting with its matriarch .

title: Solar infra helps India meet record 256GW power demand
content: at the time of peak demand of 256.1 GW reached at 3.38pm, solar contributed nearly 57 GW, or about 22% of total generation . electricity generation from solar plants rose to about 81 GW around 12.30 pm, accounting for roughly one-third of the total generation of 242 GW .

title: Brics consensus on Middle East elusive because of sharp differences, govt backs Palestine cause
content: the meeting of senior foreign ministers and special envoys could not produce a consensus document . there was sharp difference of positions among members who are party to the conflict, official sources say . a chair’s statement was issued which said the Members expressed deep concern on the recent conflict in the Middle East .

title: DGCA tightens rules for foreign airlines operating in country
content: the DGCA can "revoke or suspend" a foreign airline's authorisation for lapses like not meeting safety standards . foreign airlines must set up an effective passenger grievance redressal mechanism . grounds for cancelling or revoking authorisation include ownership and control not vested with the designating country .

title: West Bengal polls: With prayers at Kali temple, Modi counters TMC veg claim
content: 'thanthania Kalibari' is one of the few abodes of goddess Kali where meat is offered as 'prasad' . the deity is worshipped as Maa Siddheshwari and is believed to have been frequented by Ramakrishna Paramahansa .

title: Row over Uttarakhand mantri son's Rajaji temple wedding
content: preparations for the wedding of cabinet minister Khajan Das's son were carried out inside the core zone of Rajaji Tiger Reserve (RTR) on Saturday, materials for a large setup - a pandal, a stage, coolers and generators - were taken into the reserve area . the forest department registered a case against the temple committee under provisions of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 .

title: From shock to 'staged': Conspiracy theories hit the ground running after White House shooting
content: conspiracy theories flooded social media, reflecting the polarised environment in which violent incidents are instantly refracted through partisan suspicion . online forums lit up with claims that the episode was "staged," the hashtag trending on X . the swirl of speculation-unsubstantiated and often contradictory-underscored an erosion of trust in institutions and official accounts .

title: Govt looks at relief measures for airlines as US-Iran war continues

title: NIA to probe recovery of 79 crude bombs in Bengal’s Bhangar
content: central govt is of the opinion that a Scheduled Offence has been committed in the instant case . the aforesaid case is related to the illegal storage and handling of explosives and crude bombs to endanger the lives and property of citizens . a team from north Kashipur police station raided an abandoned house near a burial ground at Poilepara .

title: Firing on Trump was 'inevitable', mood 'similar' here: Maharashtra Cong MLA
content: Vijay Wadettiwar said the firing at the white house correspondents' association dinner was inevitable . he refrained from naming any neta or constitutional post . "this should not have happened to someone holding such a high offii," he said .

title: Isro plans to send civilians with STEM backgrounds to space
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More
